channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump signs a plan for reciprocal tariffs on US trading partners, ushering in economic uncertainty
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday rolled out his plan to increase U.S. tariffs to match the tax rates that other countries charge on imports, possibly triggering a broader economic confrontation with allies and rivals alike as Trump hopes to eliminate any trade imbalances.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. Tina Smith of Minnesota won’t seek reelection, dealing blow to Democrats’ hopes to take Senate
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — U.S. Sen. Tina Smith of Minnesota said Thursday that she won't seek reelection, dealing a blow to her fellow Democrats' hopes of retaking the Senate in 2026.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz’s list of ’woke’ science includes self-driving cars and solar eclipses
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, says the National Science Foundation has given money to thousands of “woke DEI” studies. Researchers say that’s misrepresenting science and disparaging important research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration was set to purchase $400 million worth of armored Teslas
item.description: That’s according to a public State Department procurement document. It comes as ethics experts raise conflict of interest questions about the chief executive of Tesla, Elon Musk, who is a top White House official.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Ways and Means chair calls for de-weaponization, overhaul of IRS after 'lawless' behavior
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: Ways & Means Chair Jason Smith calls for an overhaul of the IRS, demanding it be de-weaponized, and telling Fox News Digital that “business as usual at the IRS is unacceptable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate panel advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick to lead the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate Judiciary Committee voted along party lines on Thursday to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, pushing past Democratic concerns that he would operate as a loyalist for the president and target perceived adversaries of the White Hou
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesotans, other politicians react to Sen. Tina Smith's decision not to run again
item.description: Minnesota politicians from both sides of the aisle have weighed in on the news that U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, who has served in the Senate since 2018, is retiring after next year.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Robert F Kennedy Jr. to serve as Trump's Health secretary
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Health and Human Services (HHS) secretary in President Donald Trump's cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Tina Smith (D) will not run for reelection in 2026, opening up a seat in the Democratic-leaning state, she announced Thursday. Smith said in a video posted to the social platform X that she has “loved” her job as a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard as Trump's director of national intelligence after Republicans fall in line
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard was sworn in as President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence on Wednesday shortly after she was confirmed by the Senate, where Republicans who had initially questioned her experience and judgment fell in line behind her nomination.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans advance anti-abortion legislation during control of Minnesota House
item.description: Republicans have control of the Minnesota House at least through a special election in March, giving them a chance to advance abortion legislation that would have a tougher path in a tied chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Pro-Trump California sheriff expected to run for governor
item.description: Speculation is mounting that Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, an outspoken supporter of President Trump, will throw his hat into the race for California Governor next year.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bad landlords target of bill that provides tools to help Las Vegas tenants
item.description: Nevada cities and counties don't have a lot of options when a landlord ignores poor conditions that are putting tenants in danger. Lawmakers could use AB211 to change that.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to advance Trump's agenda
item.description: House Republicans on Wednesday released a blueprint they intend to use to advance President Trump’s legislative agenda, breaking through an impasse that had stymied the conference for days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: AG Bondi holds press conference as concerns rise over DOGE
item.description: Her remarks come as President Trump has faced criticism over the decision to fire several DOJ prosecutors and FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump upends U.S. policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a dramatic prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Fogel release a sign of Trump, Putin's 'great friendship': Witkoff
item.description: Trump and Putin had a "great friendship, and I think now it's going to continue," special envoy Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump made millions on meme coin, over 800K people lost money: Report
item.description: The price of $Trump has dropped to around $17, resulting in cumulative losses of $2 billion for several investors.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: With federal money at risk, Minnesota’s public colleges stress high stakes of state budget debate
item.description: The University of Minnesota and Minnesota State Colleges and Universities systems are hoping for extra allocations from the Legislature this year. They know it’s a challenge given the state’s leaner budget.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What’s next for Jake Sullivan, a Minnesotan and Biden’s former National Security Advisor
item.description: As National Security Advisor, Jake Sullivan helped shape Biden’s foreign policy agenda and was involved in the U.S. response to events like the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota nonprofits ride waves of tumult from Trump's spending freeze attempts
item.description: President Donald Trump’s federal spending freeze was recently blocked by a judge, but the tumult leaves Minnesota’s nearly 9,000 nonprofits and tax-exempt entities that receive federal money wondering what’s next.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gracie Stockton, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Trump seeks to expand his influence over arts and culture with Kennedy Center moves
item.description: After dominating U.S. politics for nearly a decade, President Trump now appears to be setting his sights on the country’s cultural landscape.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration targets Education Department research arm in latest cuts
item.description: The Institute of Education Sciences (IES) is responsible for gathering data on a wide range of topics, including research-backed teaching practices and the state of U.S. student achievement.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta and Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to break up Nevada's biggest agency could bring lower health care costs
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo is no fan of big government, and a plan he announced in January's State of the State address takes aim at Nevada's biggest agency, the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
